The Guava plant (Psidium guajava) is a tropical tree or shrub native to the Americas, known for its edible fruits, leaves, and other parts. The health benefits of Guava include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been linked to improved immune function, digestive health, and cardiovascular well-being. The bioactive constituents of Guava include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Guava include teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried leaves and fruit extracts used in traditional medicine and dietary supplements.
